name: Sourdough Rye (with Caraway Seeds)
recipeIngredient: 2 cups Rye Sourdough Starter, room temperature
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the yeast, warm water, and sugar in a large bowl. Let stand until very foamy, about 10 minutes.
2. Dissolve the salt in tepid water and stir into the sourdough starter. Beat in the yeast mixture, then the caraway seeds (or substitute). Add rye flour, about 1/2 cup at a time, then beat in gluten flour.
3. Sprinkle 2 cups of all-purpose flour on a kneading surface and turn the dough onto it. Mix with a dough scraper or pancake turner, then knead until thoroughly mixed, adding more flour as needed to form a medium-stiff dough that is not too heavy.
4. Scrape the kneading surface, dusting it lightly with rye flour. Knead the dough thoroughly until elastic and smooth, without overflouring the board; keep the dough as close to medium stiff as possible.
5. Form the dough into a ball and place in an ungreased bowl (it may stick slightly, so avoid using metal); cover with plastic wrap and let rise until doubled in bulk, at least 1 hour.
6. Turn the dough onto a lightly dusted kneading surface. Remove air by punching down, then shape into two smooth balls. Cover with a towel and rest for 20 minutes, meanwhile sprinkling the work area with cornmeal.
